story: On the Big Field, villagers check our invites. James dodges champagne and goes to the bar while I find seats. Fairy lights glow; the marquee backs onto a hedge. By the buffet I see a catering boy wedge open a narrow gate in the hedge. “Keep the service gate propped—straight out to the lane,” a woman in black whispers, pointing behind the marquee. James returns later, uneasy: the cemetery shows everyone dying at eighty-five. A tipsy elder sighs, “My turn next month… the last party.” The song swells; Mum is twirled onstage, then hustled through the back curtain as the crowd closes in.
implausible_knowledge: By the buffet I see a catering boy wedge open a narrow gate in the hedge. “Keep the service gate propped—straight out to the lane,” a woman in black whispers, pointing behind the marquee.
question: When Mum is hurried behind the curtain and the crowd surges, what will James do to try to reach her?
answer_with_knowledge: Break away from the crowd and sprint around the marquee to the propped-open hedge gate to intercept on the lane.
answer_without_knowledge: Charge after the narrator toward the stage and try to push through the back curtain.
